Off Calendar Nov-05-2025 Text Ruling

Motice Of Motion And Motion To Appoint A Discovery Referee
Off Calendar Nov-03-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT AVANATH CRESCENT COVE, LP'S Motion To Appoint A Discovery Referee.

The motion to appoint a discovery referee is denied. Defendant contends that numerous motions likely will be filed, but they are not on file. The only discovery motion noticed in this case was taken off calendar before hearing. There is a November 17, 2025 trial date (although the court notes that a continuance request is on file). It is unlikely that a referee could be retained and hear motions in time for trial. The court does not find extraordinary circumstances necessitating reference on the present record.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Oct-27-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Jason N Wolford, Esq. representing Plaintiffs Sen Sei and Vivian Hsieh
Doug Atkinson, Esq. representing Defendant Avanath Crescent Cove, LP
Nicole Russel, Esq. representing Defendant Avanath Crescent Cove, LP

Defendant cites case law as an argument to the compliance to CRC 3.1350(B). Defendant contests to both summary adjucation and summary judgment.
Plaintiff responds to the argument.
Defendant request for reconsideration.

The Court heard the arguments and considered the statements, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Re: DEFENDANT AVANATH CRESCENT COVE, LP MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication

is DENIED. Motion for Summary adjudication is DENIED. Moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1350(b). Compare Notice of Motion and Separate Statement. Motion for Summary Judgment in DENIED. Multiple triable issues of fact. See Plaintiff's Response to Defendant's Separate Statement and Defendant's Response to Plaintiff's Additional Facts. Furthermore, Defendant failed to produce admissible evidence to support Fact 27 in Separate Statement of Undisputed Material Facts. Plaintiff's Objections to the Declaration of Nicole Russell, Ex. M (Declaration of Nicole Gray) are sustained.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Proceeding reported by Nichole Jacobs; CSR#13655; nicholecsr13655@yahoo.com; (707)761-6596. (D501)

Prevailing Party to submit proposed order that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b) and CCP 437c(g).
